steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage explains that ‘new batteries can't be put in once the old ones no longer work.’ The blank must describe the action of swapping old batteries for new ones.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'rated': means evaluated or scored — has nothing to do with exchanging batteries. ✗
  • Choice B — 'invented': means created for the first time — unrelated to battery exchange. ✗
  • Choice C — 'prevented': means stopped from happening — opposite of the intended meaning. ✗
  • Choice D — 'replaced': means substituted with something new — directly matches the idea that old batteries cannot be swapped for new ones. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
